## [00:00 - 03:02]

So in this lesson, I just wanted to give you a quick overview of the internal launch timeline for the pre-prelaunch, the prelaunch and the open cart. Now earlier in PLF, we went through all of those, we went through the pre-pre, we went through the prelaunch, and we went through the open cart period. And so this is just shown in context of the internal launch. So the internal launch is where your launch is just your list. So first we have the pre-pre. So that is typically, it could be anywhere from one to four weeks. We'll spend in pre-pre. I mean you can start seeding your launch long before four weeks, but I really start to think about it ramping up in this one to four weeks. And then you have the classic sideways sales letter, and that's the pre-launch time. And there, you know, we'll release video one, video two, and video three. Again, they don't have to be videos, let's just call them PLC one, two, and three. And this pre-launch is typically six to ten days. Now the beautiful thing about the internal launch is you control the thing from start to finish. When you're in a joint venture launch, which we'll be getting to, joint venture launches are great, but they're more complicated. And one of the things, they're more complicated for a lot of reasons, but you have less control because if you're, depending on your partner's mailing for you, then you have to give them more time. So often this pre-launch, in an internal launch, it could be more like in the six to seven day range. In a JV launch, they usually stretch out 10, 11, 12 days because you have to give your partner's more time. In your internal launch, on day one, you're mailing. You're mailing. But in a JV launch, you're hoping your partner's mail, but a lot of times they take two or three days to mail, so you usually have to stretch this out a little bit longer. But typically, in that pre-launch, let's say it's seven days long or eight days long, and just on day one, you might mail, and day three, you might mail, and day five, you might mail. And then maybe on day seven is when you would open cart. Now, if you watched our open cart video, you know that I'm a big fan of five to seven days. So that means that, again, the pre-pre is sort of, it can vary quite a bit. It's not so precise, but if you look at this entire thing from here to here, it's going to be 11 to 17 days. So it would be more like 11 if you did six and five. If you went really on the long end, it'd be like seven and 10.

## [03:02 - 06:01]

It'd be 17 days. So here, you open cart. That's your sales video. And go watch the open cart video in the whole entire open cart module, but you're doing all kinds of things. You're mailing every single day during that open cart. So you'll notice here that if this is a six, say this is a six or seven days, that doesn't mean you release your third video the day before cart. You go one, three, five, and maybe you'd go seven, day seven, and then maybe day 12. And these can vary, you know, there is flexibility in here. It's not exact science. There's flexibility. And part of it, you can look at the days of week. So different markets have different days of week. And most markets, you don't want to be releasing a video or opening or closing on a Saturday. Usually not the best day. You probably, I often will avoid Mondays to be mailing PLC, but frankly, I've opened cart many times on Monday and closed on Friday. So I don't worry so much about days a week, but I do tend to watch out for Saturday. But you know, there are some markets that Saturday works great for. For example, the dating market, which is usually for men. And I know that it tends to be responsive market on Saturday because Saturday is like a typical date night in at least here in the US. And these guys are at home and they don't have a date. They're looking at their email. That's a great day to be selling something about how to get a date. So different markets are different. You also, as you're planning this out, you want to look at things like the time of the year. So like January is a great time for things like diets or personal development or goal setting. There are some markets that are great in the summer. There are some markets that die in the summer. You also want to look at holidays, like the end of year holidays. They can take over everything and it can be really hard to sell in the last two weeks of December. But there are some markets where that's a really good time to sell. You also want to look at big things like the Super Bowl. The Super Bowl in the US could not, probably not a great day to be closing your cart on that Sunday because people are distracted or even things like elections. So as you plan out your year and you're looking for which 11 or 17 days, just look at holidays and think about time of year. For instance, when I was selling stuff out at the stock market, usually October, late September or October, was a great time because that's when the market starts to get more volatile in a typical year. So this is just an overview of the internal launch timeframe. Again, pre-pre is pretty wide open and you have some flexibility here, but this gives you a way to plan out how you're going to do this and what type of timeframes you're going to use for your launch.
